trajectory.description: Observation: Fine-tuned model gets 0/20 on val with distances 80-130px. Training metrics show only 1 point - logging broken. Analysis: Model may not have trained well. Need to submit eval to get test score, then debug. The coordinate conversion seems correct (model outputs 0-1000, we scale to image dims), but predictions are systematically off. Decision: Submit eval with current checkpoint to get baseline, then investigate training quality and fix issues.

Observation: Model outputs bare coordinates like '840,65' or '(862,93)' without <|point_start|> tags. predict.py parsing fails on 1504/1581 samples. Analysis: The parsing is too strict - needs to handle all output formats. Also the val_test.py debug is confusing 'norm' vs 'abs' - the target coords in the data ARE absolute pixels, not normalized. Decision: 1) Rewrite predict.py with much more robust parsing, 2) Test on full val set, 3) Then retrain with better approach.

trajectory.command:
python -c "
import json, os
from transformers import AutoProcessor
from qwen_vl_utils import process_vision_info

processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained('/data/base_model/Qwen2.5-VL-3B-Instruct', min_pixels=256*28*28, max_pixels=1280*28*28)

# Test chat template format
messages = [
    {'role': 'user', 'content': [{'type': 'text', 'text': 'Test prompt'}]},
    {'role': 'assistant', 'content': [{'type': 'text', 'text': '(500,300)'}]}
]
text = processor.apply_chat_template(messages, tokenize=False, add_generation_prompt=False)
print('TEMPLATE:')
print(repr(text))
print()

# Tokenize to see token IDs
tokens = processor.tokenizer.encode(text, add_special_tokens=False)
print(f'Total tokens: {len(tokens)}')

# Check target tokens
target_tokens = processor.tokenizer.encode('(500,300)', add_special_tokens=False)
print(f'Target tokens: {target_tokens}')
print(f'Target decoded: {processor.tokenizer.decode(target_tokens)}')

im_end = processor.tokenizer.encode('<|im_end|>', add_special_tokens=False)
print(f'im_end tokens: {im_end}')
"
